title: Phyllitis ruta-muraria
taxonomy: Phyllitis ruta-muraria is a species of fern in the family Aspleniaceae. It is commonly known as the wall-rue or rustyback fern. This species is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. Phyllitis ruta-muraria is a small to medium-sized fern that grows in rocky or grassy areas, often in shady conditions. It is considered a rare and endangered species in some parts of its range.
uses: Phyllitis ruta-muraria, also known as winter cress, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It has been employed as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. In some cultures, the plant has been used to treat digestive problems, including nausea and diarrhea. The plant's leaves and stems have been used in teas and infusions to help reduce inflammation and ease pain. Phyllitis ruta-muraria has also been used in folk remedies to treat skin conditions and wounds.
